data.title: Eureka Acquisition Extends SPAC Deadline with Sponsor Financing
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Eureka Acquisition Corp Class A ( (EURK) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 2, 2026, Eureka Acquisition Corp deposited a $150,000 monthly extension fee into its trust account, funded by its sponsor Hercules Capital Management Corp, allowing the SPAC to extend its deadline to complete an initial business combination from January 3, 2026 to February 3, 2026. In connection with this payment, the company issued a $150,000 unsecured, interest-free promissory note to the sponsor on January 5, 2026, which is repayable upon the completion of a business combination or the company’s expiry and may be converted by the sponsor into private units at $10 per unit; a similar unsecured, interest-free promissory note for up to $300,000 was issued to the sponsor on January 6, 2026 to provide working capital until a deal is completed, also convertible into units. These financing steps extend Eureka’s runway to secure a target and provide operating liquidity, while potentially increasing the sponsor’s equity stake through conversion of the notes, with any resulting units subject to transfer restrictions until after the initial business combination.

More about Eureka Acquisition Corp Class A

Eureka Acquisition Corp is a Cayman Islands-exempted special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) formed to complete an initial business combination, typically by merging with or acquiring an operating business, with its public shareholders’ funds held in a trust account until such a transaction is completed.

Average Trading Volume: 4,328

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $53.32M

data.title: Thryv Holdings adopts executive retention bonus program
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Thryv Holdings ( (THRY) ).

On January 5, 2026, Thryv Holdings, Inc. approved a one-time cash retention bonus program for certain critical employees, including President Grant Freeman, Chief Operations & Customer Success Officer and Executive Vice President John Wholey, and Chief Legal Officer & Human Resources head Lesley Bolger. Under the plan, each participating executive will receive a retention bonus equal to 50% of their annual base salary, paid in two installments: 60% of the bonus after August 31, 2026, and the remaining 40% after August 31, 2027, with each payment contingent on continued employment and satisfactory performance, underscoring the company’s effort to secure leadership stability over the next two years.

The most recent analyst rating on (THRY) stock is a Hold with a $13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Thryv Holdings stock, see the THRY Stock Forecast page.

Technical indicators suggest bearish momentum, while valuation appears reasonable. Positive earnings call insights on SaaS growth and AI integration are tempered by concerns over subscriber growth and partner performance.

Chart Industries ( (GTLS) ) has shared an announcement.

Chart Industries’ board of directors has appointed Chief Human Resources Officer Gerry Vinci as president of the company, effective January 6, 2026, and decided not to name an interim chief executive officer. data.title: Heron Therapeutics Reports Strong Preliminary Q4 2025 Revenue
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Heron Therapeutics ( (HRTX) ) has shared an announcement.

On January 9, 2026, Heron Therapeutics reported preliminary, unaudited net revenue of approximately $40.5 million for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025, and about $154.9 million for full-year 2025, underscoring continued momentum across its acute care and oncology supportive care portfolio. Within Q4, CINVANTI remained the largest revenue contributor at roughly $22.9 million, while ZYNRELEF generated about $12.5 million and delivered the strongest quarter-over-quarter growth in the portfolio, rising around 35% versus the third quarter, with APONVIE and SUSTOL contributing approximately $3.8 million and $1.3 million, respectively; the solid top-line performance and particularly strong uptake of ZYNRELEF highlight progress in Heron’s acute care franchise and suggest strengthening commercial execution heading into the company’s final audited results.

The most recent analyst rating on (HRTX) stock is a Buy with a $6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Heron Therapeutics stock, see the HRTX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HRTX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HRTX is a Neutral.

Heron Therapeutics faces significant financial challenges, including high leverage and negative profitability, which weigh heavily on its overall stock score. While there are positive developments in product growth and strategic initiatives, these are offset by valuation concerns and technical indicators suggesting potential short-term volatility. The recent board appointment is a positive step but has a limited impact on the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on HRTX stock, click here.

More about Heron Therapeutics

Heron Therapeutics, Inc., based in Cary, N.C., is a commercial-stage biotechnology company focused on developing and commercializing therapeutics that improve medical care for acute care and oncology patients. Its portfolio includes ZYNRELEF for postoperative pain, APONVIE and CINVANTI for prevention of chemotherapy- and surgery-related nausea and vomiting, and SUSTOL for chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting, leveraging proprietary drug delivery technologies to advance standards of care in these segments.

data.title: Venu Holding Expands Aramark Partnership and Raises Capital
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Venu Holding Corporation ( (VENU) ) is now available.

On January 5, 2026, Venu Holding Corporation amended its existing letter of intent with Aramark Sports and Entertainment Services, expanding Aramark’s role as exclusive provider of food, beverage, catering, concession, retail, custodial, grounds, and facility maintenance services to cover two additional amphitheaters the company plans to build in El Paso and the greater Houston area, on the same terms as its Ford Amphitheater in Colorado Springs and Sunset Amphitheaters in McKinney and Tulsa, and extending Aramark’s right of first refusal on services at future venues as long as it retains its preferred or converted common stock holdings. In tandem with this operational expansion, Aramark committed an additional $10.005 million equity investment via 667 new shares of Series B 4% Cumulative Convertible Preferred Stock to be issued in two tranches in 2026, prompting Venu to file on January 6, 2026 an amendment to increase the authorized Series B Preferred Stock from 675 to 1,342 shares without changing its rights or preferences, and to confirm that Aramark’s registration rights for underlying common stock also apply to these new shares, strengthening Venu’s capital base and deepening its strategic alliance with a major venue services provider.

The most recent analyst rating on (VENU) stock is a Hold with a $8.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Venu Holding Corporation stock, see the VENU Stock Forecast page.
